title: leonotis sexdentata
intro: Leonotis sexdentata, also known as the Lion's Tail, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems contain essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections. In herbal preparations, the dried leaves and flowers of Leonotis sexdentata can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
